mnemonics: GROUP BY: Get Ready, Organize Your Packets, By Your Variables
keyPoints: GROUP BY clause is used to group rows that have the same values in specified columns into summary rows.
It is often used with aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN) to perform a calculation on each group of data.
The HAVING clause is used to filter groups based on a condition, similar to how WHERE filters individual rows.
commonMistakes: Using an aggregate function in the WHERE clause instead of the HAVING clause to filter groups.
Including a column in the SELECT list that is not part of the GROUP BY clause and is not an aggregate function.
Forgetting that WHERE filters rows before grouping, while HAVING filters groups after aggregation.
